scope: Complete coverage of GitHub Copilot platform capabilities, architecture, features, and best practices across all documentation sections

1_copilot_overview_and_fundamentals.what_is_copilot: GitHub Copilot is an AI coding assistant that helps developers write code faster and with less effort by providing code suggestions, answering questions, and automating coding tasks. Research shows it increases developer productivity and accelerates software development.
foundational_capabilities: Get code suggestions as you type in your IDE (inline suggestions)
Chat with Copilot to get help with code (Copilot Chat)
Ask for help on the command line (GitHub Copilot CLI)
Organize and share context with Copilot Spaces
Generate pull request descriptions and summaries
Autonomous code changes and PR creation (Copilot coding agent)
supported_platforms: IDEs: Visual Studio Code, Visual Studio, JetBrains IDEs (IntelliJ, PyCharm, WebStorm, etc.), Eclipse, Xcode
1_copilot_overview_and_fundamentals.fundamental_constraint: Copilot is an AI coding assistant designed specifically for coding-related work. It is not designed to respond to prompts unrelated to coding and technology, and should not replace developer expertise—developers remain in charge.

copilot_free.features: Limited access to select Copilot features to explore AI-powered coding assistance
copilot_free.monthly_limits: 50 messages/month in IDE, 2000 completions/month for inline suggestions, 50 premium requests/month
copilot_free.models: Claude Haiku 4.5, Gemini 2.5 Pro/3 Flash/3 Pro/3.1 Pro, GPT-4.1, GPT-5 mini, Grok Code Fast 1

copilot_student.features: Unlimited completions, premium model access in chat, coding agent access, premium request allowance
copilot_student.eligibility: Verified student status through GitHub Education

copilot_pro.features: Unlimited completions, premium models in chat, coding agent, 300 premium requests/month
models: Gemini models (2.5 Pro, 3 Pro, 3.1 Pro, 3 Flash)

copilot_pro_plus.features: All Copilot Pro features PLUS large allowance of premium requests, full access to ALL available models
copilot_pro_plus.target_users: AI power users wanting maximum capabilities and advanced model access

copilot_business.target: Organizations on GitHub Free, Team, or Enterprise Cloud
admin_capabilities: Manage policies for Copilot in organization
Review usage data and user activity

premium_requests_model.concept: Premium requests use advanced models and count against monthly allowance. Allowance renews first day of month.
premium_requests_model.pro_plus_tier: 1500 premium requests/month (includes increased GitHub Models rate limits)
premium_requests_model.additional_purchases: Users can purchase additional requests at $0.04 USD per request (available on Free, Pro, Pro+, Business, Enterprise plans)

billing_notes.trial_mechanics: 30-day Copilot Pro trial requires payment method; automatically converts to paid plan unless cancelled
billing_notes.cancellation: Effective at end of current billing cycle
billing_notes.auto_cancellation_on_org_assignment: If assigned Copilot Business/Enterprise seat, personal plan automatically cancels with prorated refund
billing_notes.prorated_billing: If already billed by GitHub, Copilot charges included in next bill; pro-rated for initial period

copilot_chat.definition: Interactive chat interface for asking coding-related questions
availability: IDEs: VS Code, Visual Studio, JetBrains, Eclipse, Xcode
capabilities: Use slash commands for specialized tasks
Use chat participants for focused context (/workspace, @project, etc.)
Use skills to accomplish specific tasks
Reference files, code snippets, symbols in prompts
copilot_chat.context_management: Uses chat history for context; supports threads for separate conversations; users can delete irrelevant context

copilot_coding_agent.definition: Autonomous AI agent that can independently make code changes to complete tasks
capabilities: Assign GitHub issues to Copilot agent
Iterates on errors until task complete
Integration with external applications via MCP servers

agent_mode.best_for: Complex, multi-step tasks requiring autonomous execution and error handling
agent_mode.control_level: Copilot determines necessary files and steps; you review/approve PRs

copilot_coding_agent.third_party_agents: Public preview for third-party specialized agents alongside Copilot coding agent
copilot_coding_agent.availability: Copilot Pro+, Copilot Business, Copilot Enterprise (NOT included in Free, Student, Pro)

inline_suggestions.definition: Autocomplete-style code suggestions that appear as you type
availability: IDEs: VS Code, Visual Studio, JetBrains, Azure Data Studio, Xcode, Vim/Neovim, Eclipse
capabilities: Next edit suggestions (VS Code, Xcode, Eclipse) - predicts next edit location

copilot_edits.definition: Make changes across multiple files from single Copilot Chat prompt
copilot_edits.availability: Visual Studio Code, Visual Studio, JetBrains IDEs

edit_mode.control: You choose files Copilot can modify, provide context each turn, approve changes per iteration
edit_mode.best_for: Quick, specific updates to defined file sets; full control over LLM requests

agent_mode.control: Copilot autonomously determines files, code changes, terminal commands; iterates on errors
agent_mode.best_for: Complex tasks, multi-step work, external integrations via MCP

copilot_code_review.definition: AI-generated code review suggestions for pull requests
availability: IDEs: VS Code ('Review selection'), JetBrains, Eclipse, Xcode

copilot_pull_request_summaries.definition: AI-generated summaries of PR changes, impacted files, and review focus points

copilot_custom_instructions.definition: Provide contextual details to enhance Copilot Chat responses with preferences, tools, requirements
copilot_custom_instructions.impact: Guides Copilot behavior without modifying system prompts

copilot_memory_public_preview.definition: Copilot deduces and stores useful information about repository for future use
copilot_memory_public_preview.used_by: Copilot coding agent and code review for improved output quality

copilot_spaces.definition: Organize and centralize relevant content (code, docs, specs) for context-grounded responses
copilot_spaces.benefit: Improved answer relevance through structured context organization

github_spark_public_preview.definition: Build and deploy full-stack applications using natural-language prompts
github_spark_public_preview.integration: Seamless GitHub platform integration for advanced development

copilot_github_desktop.definition: Automatically generate commit messages and descriptions based on changes
copilot_github_desktop.benefit: Faster commit creation with AI-generated descriptions

4_custom_agents_critical.agent_definition: Specialized versions of Copilot tailored to unique workflows, coding conventions, use cases. Act like customized teammates following team standards.

required_fields: prompt: Custom instructions defining behavior and expertise
optional_fields: tools: Specific tools agent can access (defaults to all available)

platform_availability.cross_environment: Properties may function differently between environments

best_practices: Encode conventions and frameworks directly into prompts
Define specific tools accessible to each agent
Use relative links for repository references
Add descriptive alt text to images
Structure agent scope (e.g., 'limited to README files only')

5_github_copilot_cli_critical.definition: Command-line interface for using Copilot from within terminal
core_capabilities: Interact with GitHub.com (list PRs, create issues, etc.
5_github_copilot_cli_critical.availability: Included in all plans (Free, Pro, Pro+, Business, Enterprise)
5_github_copilot_cli_critical.note: Full CLI documentation details (modes, commands, authentication) available in official docs

6_prompt_engineering_techniques.fundamental_principle: Structuring requests so Copilot easily understands and responds effectively

start_general_get_specific: Give broad description of goal first
Enables Copilot to understand context before constraints
give_examples: Unit tests can serve as examples for TDD
break_complex_into_simple: Ask Copilot to accomplish them sequentially
Improves success rate for complex operations
avoid_ambiguity: Be specific about which file or function
experiment_and_iterate: Refine prompts if first attempt unsatisfactory
keep_history_relevant: Use threads for new tasks (separate conversations)
Delete requests no longer helpful for context
follow_good_coding_practices: Ask Copilot to help implement these

7_best_practices_for_usage.understand_strengths_weaknesses: Know what Copilot does best and when to validate carefully

copilot_chat_best_for: Generating large code sections with iteration
Acting as specific persona (e.g., review code as Senior Dev)

verify_copilot_work: Review carefully for functionality and security
Check for IP/license concerns related to public code
optimize_context: Start new conversations for new tasks
Specify repositories, files, symbols in GitHub Chat
Use keywords to focus IDE Chat on specific context
7_best_practices_for_usage.rewrite_prompts: If Copilot not helpful, try rephrasing or splitting into multiple prompts
7_best_practices_for_usage.multiple_suggestions: Copilot may offer multiple suggestions; use keyboard shortcuts to compare

claude_models: Claude Haiku 4.5 (Free, Student, Pro, Pro+, Business, Enterprise)
Claude Opus 4.6 fast mode (Pro+ only, preview)
gemini_models: Gemini 2.5 Pro (Student, Pro, Pro+, Business, Enterprise)
Gemini 3 Flash (Student, Pro, Pro+, Business, Enterprise)
Gemini 3 Pro (Student, Pro, Pro+, Business, Enterprise)
Gemini 3.1 Pro (Student, Pro, Pro+, Business, Enterprise)
gpt_models: GPT-4.1 (Free, Student, Pro, Pro+, Business, Enterprise)
GPT-5 mini (Free, Student, Pro, Pro+, Business, Enterprise)
GPT-5.1-Codex, GPT-5.1-Codex-Mini, GPT-5.1-Codex-Max (Student, Pro, Pro+, Business, Enterprise)
GPT-5.2, GPT-5.2-Codex (Student, Pro, Pro+, Business, Enterprise)
other: Grok Code Fast 1 (Free, Student, Pro, Pro+, Business, Enterprise)

8_model_availability_and_routing.auto_model_selection: Copilot can automatically select appropriate models based on task type
8_model_availability_and_routing.model_context_protocol: MCP integration with custom servers can provide additional models/tools

model_context_protocol.definition: Protocol for integrating external tools and services with Copilot

9_integration_and_mcp.mcp_in_custom_agents: Custom agents can include mcp-server property for server configurations
9_integration_and_mcp.agent_mode_external_integration: Agent mode in Copilot Edits can integrate with external applications via MCP
9_integration_and_mcp.use_cases: Access specialized tools, extend capabilities, integrate domain-specific functionality

organizational_features.policy_management: Manage Copilot policies in organization or enterprise
organizational_features.access_management: Specify which organization members can use Copilot
organizational_features.usage_data: Review usage data to inform adoption and access decisions
organizational_features.audit_logs: Review audit logs for actions taken and by which users
organizational_features.content_exclusion: Exclude files from Copilot (files won't be available to suggestions)

11_security_privacy_and_compliance.data_privacy: GitHub Copilot handles code data according to GitHub Privacy Statement

code_reference_matching.feature: Copilot can suggest code matching public code
code_reference_matching.availability: Setting available at individual and organizational levels

11_security_privacy_and_compliance.content_exclusion: Configure Copilot to ignore specified files (e.g., sensitive data, IP)
11_security_privacy_and_compliance.audit_capabilities: Organizations can review audit logs for compliance
11_security_privacy_and_compliance.enterprise_focus: Copilot Enterprise includes enhanced compliance and governance features
11_security_privacy_and_compliance.ip_protection: Users should review suggestions for similarity to public code

copilot_chat_skills.definition: Specialized capabilities that Copilot Chat can execute
copilot_chat_skills.availability: Available in IDE Chat (VS Code, Visual Studio) and GitHub Chat
copilot_chat_skills.usage: Users access skills through slash commands or direct invocation in chat

12_skills_and_extensibility.skill_ecosystem: GitHub and community contribute skills; users can build custom skills
12_skills_and_extensibility.npx_skills: Skills can be packaged and distributed via npm ecosystem

personal_account_setup.free_access: Copilot Free - try at no cost
personal_account_setup.teacher_maintainer_access: Free for teachers and open source maintainers under specific programs

organizational_access.copilot_business: Org owners purchase Copilot Business for team
organizational_access.member_request: Members request access via GitHub settings
organizational_access.enterprise_owned: Enterprise can enable Copilot for organizations within it

enterprise_access.assignment: Can assign to organizations or individual users/teams
enterprise_access.business_only_enterprise: Can create enterprise account just for Copilot Business without full GitHub Enterprise

common_issues: Suggestions not matching expectations - iterate and refine prompts
Poor response quality - check Copilot tool is appropriate for task
Extension installation - follow platform-specific installation guides
Rate limiting - Premium requests may be exhausted; can purchase additional
support_resources: GitHub Community Discussions for peer support
GitHub blog changelog for feature updates

copilot_architecture.model: Monolithic - single Copilot entity with variants (Chat, Agent, CLI) that share common foundation
copilot_architecture.context_passing: Built into each environment (IDE, CLI, GitHub.com) - not file-based
copilot_architecture.agent_composition: Custom agents define behavior via Markdown profiles with YAML frontmatter
copilot_architecture.state_management: Chat history in conversation threads; agent state implicit in PR/issue context
copilot_architecture.extensibility: Via MCP servers for tool integration; skills for specialized tasks
copilot_architecture.instruction_system: Custom instructions at repository, personal, and organization levels

ben_orchestration_architecture.model: Hierarchical - Ben coordinates specialist agents at .github/agents/ directory
ben_orchestration_architecture.context_passing: File-based via .github/context/ temporary JSON documents with metadata
ben_orchestration_architecture.agent_composition: .agent.md YAML frontmatter + Markdown instructions with Ben as orchestrator
ben_orchestration_architecture.state_management: Explicit via directory structure and temp doc versioning
ben_orchestration_architecture.extensibility: Self-similar - new agents added as .agent.md files in hierarchical pattern
ben_orchestration_architecture.instruction_system: Distributed instructions per agent in .agent.md files

convergences: Both use Markdown + YAML frontmatter for agent definition
Both support custom instructions/prompts for behavior tuning
Both recognize need for tool composition and external integrations
Both implement MCP as extensibility layer
Both consider context as critical design element
divergences: Copilot: monolithic + environment-specific. Ben: modular + file-based coordination
Copilot: implicit state in platform (PR, issues). Ben: explicit file-based state
Copilot: centralized context per platform. Ben: transported via JSON documents
Copilot: built-in chat/agent variants. Ben: uniform agent model with specialization
implications_for_ben: File-based coordination is novel vs Copilot's environment-bound approach - competitive advantage
Explicit state management enables better debugging and transparency
Hierarchical orchestration more scalable for many agents than monolithic
Custom instructions pattern validates use of .agent.md for agent definition
MCP integration aligns capacity - both support external tool expansion

for_ben_orchestrator: Copilot coding agent is autonomous but still requires issue assignment - consider how Ben handles task initiation
Copilot agents use profiles to define scope - validate Ben agents use similar boundaries
Custom instructions at multiple levels (repo, personal, org) - Ben should consider layering strategy
Third-party agents (preview) suggest Copilot moving toward multi-agent scenarios - aligns with Ben's vision
for_ar_upskiller: Model availability varies by plan - agents need to handle model selection gracefully
Premium requests concept - consider if Ben agents need rate-limiting awareness
Prompt engineering is critical - invest in instruction quality for custom agents
Skills ecosystem is growing - agents should understand skill-based task completion
for_agentic_workflow_researcher: Copilot's custom agent format (Markdown YAML) is becoming platform standard
MCP integration is strategic - both Copilot and Ben recognize it as key extensibility
Hierarchical task decomposition (break complex into simple) aligns with orchestration principles
Autonomous agents (coding agent) paired with human review (PR approval) is Copilot's trust model
for_ar_director_recruitment: Potential capability gap: Ben has no MCP server management specialist - Copilot integrates MCP deeply
Potential gap: No skill builder/curator - Copilot has growing skills ecosystem
Validation: Custom agent definition aligns with Ben's .agent.md approach
Validation: File-based context passing confirmed as valid pattern

skills_ecosystem_research_gaps_filled: Comprehensive model portfolio documented (Claude, Gemini, GPT families)
Custom agents as first-class citizen explained thoroughly
Public code matching and IP protection explained
Organization-level custom instructions (public preview) now in knowledge base
remaining_unknowns: Exact CLI command set and mode specifications - rate limited out of fetches
MCP server deployment patterns in practice - limited detail in Copilot docs
Custom instructions file format and execution semantics - mentioned but not detailed
Agent failure modes and recovery patterns - not documented
Performance characteristics of custom agents at scale - not in docs
underutilized_copilot_features: Copilot Spaces for context organization - overlooked in most onboarding
Agent memory (public preview) - powerful for repository knowledge but immature
Copilot Edits agent mode - more powerful than edit mode but less known
Third-party agents - still in preview, not yet mainstream

for_immediate_implementation: Validate Ben's .agent.md format against Copilot custom agent profile standard - they align well
Implement layered custom instructions similar to Copilot (repo, agent level, organization) if not already present
Ensure Ben agents have clear scope boundaries like Copilot agents do ('.limited to README files only')
Integrate MCP integration testing early - Copilot treats it as critical extensibility
for_medium_term: Design Ben's skill ecosystem curator (@skill-builder) role - Copilot has strong skills concept
Plan MCP server management patterns - no doc but essential for tool composition
Evaluate how Ben agents handle autonomous execution with human review (like Copilot agent PR workflow)
Consider memory banking system similar to Copilot Memory for repository knowledge
for_long_term: Monitor Copilot's third-party agents evolution - indicates direction of agent composition
Plan Ben's scaling strategy - Copilot's monolithic approach has limits that hierarchical beats
Evaluate Ben's advantage: file-based coordination is superior to environment-bound for transparency
Build institutional knowledge: model selection, prompt engineering, context management

sources.title: About billing for individual GitHub Copilot plans
Best practices for using GitHub Copilot
Prompt engineering for GitHub Copilot Chat

summary: Comprehensive exploration of GitHub Copilot documentation reveals 14 major areas of functionality: overview/fundamentals, pricing/licensing, features (chat, agent, inline, edits, code review, memory, Spaces, Spark), custom agents, CLI, prompt engineering, best practices, model availability, MCP integration, administration/governance, security/privacy, skills, account management, and troubleshooting. Copilot uses Markdown YAML agent profiles for custom agents with repository/personal/org configuration levels. Custom instructions guide behavior at multiple levels. MCP integration is critical for tool extension. Premium requests model manages advanced capability access. Comparison with Ben's orchestration shows convergence on agent definition format but divergence on state management (file-based vs implicit). Key gaps: exact CLI specifications (rate limited), MCP deployment patterns, custom instructions format details, agent failure modes, scaling characteristics. Actionable recommendations: validate .agent.md format alignment, implement layered custom instructions, integrate MCP testing, design skills curator role, plan memory banking system, monitor third-party agent evolution.
follow_up_questions: What are the exact GitHub Copilot CLI command specifications and mode parameters (c-ask, c-plan, c-edit, c-agent)?
What is the detailed custom instructions file format and syntax (e.g., copilot-instructions.md structure)?
How do custom agents handle failure modes and recovery in Copilot execution?
What are Copilot agent performance characteristics at scale (number of concurrent agents, throughput)?
How does Copilot Memory actually store and retrieve repository-specific knowledge internally?
What are production deployment patterns for MCP servers as Copilot extensions?
How do third-party agents coordinate with Copilot coding agent in preview?
What are detailed benchmarks of model selection across task types?
How does Ben's file-based context passing compare in latency to Copilot's environment-bound approach?
What governance/audit capabilities exist at scale for enterprise Copilot deployments?
